_doc:
Build lab/eval/purity_sample.json — hard gate (b).

Classification rule (stated so it can be argued with):
  core     = mechanistic / inner-structure interpretability: circuits, features,
             neurons, activation-space geometry, probing internals, lens methods,
             causal localization inside the model.
  adjacent = interpretability but not mechanistic: post-hoc input attribution
             (LIME/SHAP/IG/LRP/CAM), XAI surveys, position papers, and pure
             model-editing methods/evals (edit as a capability, not a finding).
  off      = not interpretability at all (base-model releases, benchmarks,
             other fields), or a corrupted OpenAlex record.

NOTE: this topical rule differs slightly from the landmark tiering in
landmarks.json — there Joao tiered FFN Key-Value Memories and Knowledge Neurons
as 'adjacent' for gate (a). Topically they are mechanistic-internals work, so
they count as core here. Flagged rather than silently reconciled.

items.title: Learning to Generate Reviews and Discovering Sentiment
items.why: sentiment neuron — first single-unit feature discovery in a language model

items.title: Locating and Editing Factual Associations in GPT
items.why: ROME: causal tracing localizes factual associations to specific MLP layers

items.why: IOI: a full circuit reverse-engineered in GPT-2 small

items.why: sparse autoencoders recover monosemantic features from the residual stream
